textByHash.0038fff12e3c2fdb02cb8bad731e33498610d464: That is no concern of yours, adventurer.
textByHash.0166d814bf025662d3077ac13d7f8f1833a172a7: We can discuss this more when you have the rest of the escape kit.
textByHash.02055f252aa74401879bb9d97be753a0e42a5f97: I'd like two Teak small tables built.
textByHash.0322bf5c8ce9397b035c45a3aba8af25d019c36b: Most of the guards will be easy. The disguise will get past them. The only guard who will be a problem will be the one at the door.
textByHash.04b2836baed32ae9d0eddcb442eeb08c80706e36: Good, you have all the basic equipment. Next to deal with the guard on the door. He is talkative, try to find a weakness in him.
textByHash.063a01f6063425e2a114c9acfda8e97632b128ec: To make the Prince look like Keli, you'll need a blonde wig and a pink skirt. You'll also want some skin paste to hide the black eye he got when they captured him.
textByHash.082e2dfeaa27ba8f5d3b0cde00c11f411827e24c: I'd like a Oak bed built.
textByHash.0e6412c9941c1b3d75b609501540d2327d029884: Good, I shall await your return with everything.
textByHash.0fc7353f2911f8f504a3e1665636ab533b45bdc3: Get some soft clay and get her to show you the key somehow. Then take the print, with bronze, to my father.
textByHash.103e7e4f74983278ba9582f6214ad83ce7acaeb5: You have the skin paint, well done. I thought you would struggle to make that.
textByHash.1167f31a77d1923f2d141bb025ca14fea36e20ba: You're back. How are things going with that guard?
textByHash.1331b8e827563f3c5760b6d1a266bb903f4d312a: You have got the skirt, good.
textByHash.1408a21b9ae2a930017e853ef92d0c755c41b003: Your employment is known to me. Now, do you know all that we need to make the break?
textByHash.149726c3e8e8f0f135d562d2a43889323eedde4c: Yes, that could work. I'd imagine three beers would do it. Why don't you give it a try?
textByHash.1da2c88053ba5470abec55c3cdcf545fa93325bd: Foolish woman! A new key will cost 15 coins.
textByHash.20b7c9fb73b5099ffa83a036208c6a9b5b1b04e6: You have rope I see, to tie up Keli. That will be the most dangerous part of the plan.
textByHash.2b397891097f651ed21e8868840c662e693adf0b: You need a wig, maybe made from wool. If you find someone who can work with wool ask them about it. There's a witch nearby may be able to help you dye it.
textByHash.359e8e879ef5dee2d419cfce3e5ddacc31f14760: My father sent this key for you. Be careful not to lose it.
textByHash.35eaf0e7c3202da54b835291a8d2eef96e9349e3: Yes, that is most important. There is no way you can get the real key. It is on a chain around Keli's neck. Almost impossible to steal.
textByHash.3a093d12232cc9b6c8d5b09f361c512516cc1079: I'd like a Oak cupboard built.
textByHash.3a7f1eb4f0ca7a9ab2b832687886bae0d458877b: Finally there's the skirt. I imagine you could just buy one from any clothes shop. Thessalia's Fine Clothes in Varrock is probably the closest.
textByHash.3b9efd2ff6ef0dbe07e5cc022753ae59d5cd5780: Well that was foolish. I can sort you out with another, but it will cost you 15 coins.
textByHash.3da1698dbdeaa4768f33051137c5df01371d79a4: You're back. How are things going?
textByHash.3e1deefba9026b5e51626099124d6c40d6cc6916: We still don't know why Keli and her bandits took the Prince. I'm hoping I can find out. The place where they imprisoned him seems a good starting point.
textByHash.41926644b2213270e0589e6e1d5ae51e66a957ed: You did it! Prince Ali is now safe again. You should head back to Al Kharid. I expect you will be well rewarded for your work.
textByHash.4900b2307dd4b5f2d82bfa7ab836e8ec1ed185b7: I'd like two Oak small tables built.
textByHash.50fdf2194d21bfd5a2b7d658b80efee52f1223db: Yes, he sent word ahead that you'd be coming. Are you aware of the plan?
textByHash.513d3abbe5e37dd7dc455fa5484bbaed749f4e81: Good work. Now, before breaking the Prince out, you'll need to find a way to deal with his personal guard. He's talkative, so try to find a weakness. Remember, we don't want any unneeded violence.
textByHash.52f7cb3d9e0e655fc1f55212e865dc10019958de: Thank you, Al-Kharid will forever owe you for your help. I think that if there is ever anything that needs to be done, you will be someone they can rely on.
textByHash.538c7f3a0c546c7805c720691c537870649d992f: Now, how are things going with that guard?
textByHash.5494c32a20d099293cd41e9c3056b184745ac9fc: Good luck with the guard. When the guard is out you can tie up Keli.
textByHash.597a919cb60135cdf1a0d6a4ab2120fdf540cfea: Great! I think that means we're ready. Go in and use some rope to tie Keli up. Once she's dealt with, use the key to free the Prince. Don't forget to give him his disguise so the guards outside don't spot him.
textByHash.63019649378d95aca0f6536abb1e77ee5beadd58: Keli killed him in front of the other guards, as a deterrent. It would probably take a lot of gold.
textByHash.66b2acfda72665d98ea4d36ee1a79742e6c1565f: I'd like a Teak bed built.
textByHash.7a867ecaa332fb62e4a4898a1ced58bfd8bbe6bf: We still need something to colour the Prince's skin lighter. There's a witch close to here. She knows about many things. She may know some way to make the skin lighter.
textByHash.7ad533df7067c6c6b7b922878af135eda72f9fc4: The wig you have got, well done.
textByHash.7f04e1b825da1c28b10027225a5abf4c59f6e523: You will still need some rope to tie up Keli, of course. I heard that there's a good rope maker around here.
textByHash.864f2b4bfce18b13a97c6b8c84176ebb7f25155a: Please excuse me, I'm rather busy.
textByHash.93f26532c5785a01da38af58cdd15cebb69a4ed5: I'd like a Mahogany cupboard built.
textByHash.9aeac939195ac4bebb7a2b47b01f4c0b244528ef: Thank you so much! Would you like a cup of tea before you go?
textByHash.9d1e4d56a1112d36a5f93bb1fbea02f54438bf1f: I'd like a Teak cupboard built.
textByHash.a078c0502e0cc1dc9f9fb3a7e7bcc387b881fce4: My suggestion is that you pretend to be interested in joining her bandits. From there, you should be able to steer the conversation towards the key.
textByHash.a3b67d468f5869329c738ea94450a9df7b619d16: I'd like a Mahogany bed built.
textByHash.a6cd885411c7de8c3f7a2cb9024e8ec9664eb6db: I'd like a Teak table built.
textByHash.a7c95ac073eba3c6a97668bb735717631d05e831: I'd like two Mahogany small tables built.
textByHash.a8a92ecde07aaee45a997c1792ff1025de201de1: I'd say that's a good summary. Now, do you have any questions for me about any part of the plan?
textByHash.ab84af2da55d7b256c8b3fb8f5c2fb4fdac76df9: It would take a lot of gold to convince him to betray Keli. She's not known to be kind to those she believes to be traitors. Perhaps there's something else you could try.
textByHash.ac3ebc738ac95d57d90bb6514e66895391f6dd07: I'd like a Wooden cupboard built.
textByHash.b29d950361002de39c013ba2a20fc06c74d544ee: You will need to get a pink skirt, same as Keli's.
textByHash.b3f54554d4284ff9f2a737a96ecba931d2a84ebe: I don't think you should. If you do the rest of the gang and Keli would attack you. The door guard should be removed first, to make it easy.
textByHash.b436a98798c3e5afc940fb2a15345bb2afcbf000: Al Kharid will forever owe you for your help in saving Prince Ali. It's good to know that we have you as a friend.
textByHash.b59f70f45badf75ba7477233bb8475ab83bcc875: You're back. Do you have everything needed yet?
textByHash.c383cbf5cc3d1f9f9180c826ddbdd97330490d0e: I'd like a Wooden bed built.
textByHash.c3cfe4675c62f4e2e1c70b813a05fbbecf727782: You could try. I don't think the emir will pay anything towards it. And we did bribe one of their guards once.
textByHash.c89b00ead48c05b85dd331cfd1125639c7a04d51: Ok now, you have all the basic equipment. What are your plans to stop the guard interfering?
textByHash.cecdb408c6bb21aa52fbcc768d260e04c9e7ca3b: For the skin paste, there's a local witch who's apparently an expert on all sorts of potions. I'm sure she could make you some. I hear she also sells dye, if you need some for the wig.
textByHash.d5edd5f430b8b3f505758333570bec8ccab050fc: I'd like a Mahogany table built.
textByHash.d67d0d4aba1ec4de3dac1b637f9413979a536c77: I'd like two Wooden small tables built.
textByHash.d6d4f6ab4ac252f39e506c7e334495efc57e7378: Only the lady Keli, can wander about outside the jail. The guards will shoot to kill if they see the prince out so we need a disguise good enough to fool them at a distance.
textByHash.d7343661646d2e75d557aec68f6d1dda2f2188e1: Foolish man! A new key will cost 15 coins.
textByHash.df5216fada3327cff90f8655bf31dc980f0ac083: Then you should take it to my father along with a bronze bar. He'll then be able to make us a copy.
textByHash.e25d9b28a4c85333299749cba53ad95fa654c7ec: I'd like a Wooden table built.
textByHash.e85864537819bce84483b10fa79c030c8aec863c: Keli keeps it on her at all times, on a chain around her neck. If you can convince her to show it to you, you might be able to use some soft clay to take an imprint.
textByHash.e9e8c3b88bf3c833e1ef43df65e6932717b9ac3a: You should try talking to him. He might give away some sort of weakness.
textByHash.ec0be868ec5660c8bae8dbddb08e0d3b57d75b13: Once you have the imprint, take it to my father along with a bronze bar. He'll then be able to make us a copy.
textByHash.ec9ecc67fde4943f01ff55aa366fb4e1d23719ab: Well you'd better get on it then. We need him out of the way.
textByHash.eeb5b3270a87a982c52a7d711cf1db99daf6a986: I'd like a Oak table built.
textByHash.ef2c8778aff5bac96101968fe813fa3d40c46d24: Well, speaking to him may find a weakness he has. See if there's something that could stop him bothering us.
textByHash.f149a20791f61343b2a0ad4c7df1132430e47bfb: I don't think that's a good idea. Any violence could put the Prince at risk.
textByHash.f658f531649c9d13e04786285126f4cf74b191d8: Then come back to me when you do.
textByHash.f76b66b8b2454554ccec4dd5cd1b9bbe5c947f9d: Well, that's possible. These guards do like a drink. I would think it will take at least 3 beers to do it well. You would probably have to do it all at the same time too. The effects of the local beer wear off quickly.
textByHash.fa32368348d20d94dde3adcaea6ebbe819cde717: Do you have any other questions about the plan?
textByHash.fc83dc766ec799b003786ef37569077132e103c6: There's an old sailor in the village who makes rope. Perhaps he could make you a wig. Don't forget to dye it once you have one.
